heading: Victims of fire
label: 290a Victims of fire
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Federal Fire Prevention and Control Act of 1974 (which is classified principally to chapter 49 (§ 2201 et seq.) of Title 15), and not as a part of the Public Health Service Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Research on burns, burn injuries, and rehabilitation
children.label: a Research on burns, burn injuries, and rehabilitation

children.text: sponsor and encourage the establishment throughout the Nation of twenty-five additional burn centers, which shall comprise separate hospital facilities providing specialized burn treatment and including research and teaching programs and twenty-five additional burn units, which shall comprise specialized facilities in general hospitals used only for burn victims;
provide training and continuing support of specialists to staff the new burn centers and burn units;
sponsor and encourage the establishment of ninety burn programs in general hospitals which comprise staffs of burn injury specialists;
provide special training in emergency care for burn victims;
augment sponsorship of research on burns and burn treatment;
administer and support a systematic program of research concerning smoke inhalation injuries; and
sponsor and support other research and training programs in the treatment and rehabilitation of burn injury victims.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: b Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For purposes of this section, there are authorized to be appropriated not to exceed $5,000,000 for the fiscal year ending and not to exceed $8,000,000 for the fiscal year ending .June 30, 1975June 30, 1976
